text:
Once upon a time in the quiet town of Fernlake, there was a wise old owl named Olga. She had fluffy white feathers like clouds and keen eyes that could see things coming long before they happened.

One autumn afternoon, a young fox named Rupert came flying down onto her branch.

"Pardon me," said Rupert. "I'm lost. Surely you must have something I could consult?"

Just then, Old OWL lips twitched.

"I know a little about everything," Olga replied gently. "But you know... wisdom doesn?”

"I think I do," said Rupert.

"And what's that?" Olga asked with an amused glint in her eye.

"A desire to do good."

"Well," she smiled. "Actually."

Olga took a deep breath. She recounted a story about a wicked wizard once who wanted to turn frogs into mice. There was endless suffering among frog ponds. Olga set out brave warriors led by herself. They fought day after dreadful day, saving countless frogs
